license_label: Minnesota Residential Building Contractor License Number
license_notes: Residential contractors need Residential Building Contracting license from MN Dept. of Labor and Industry. Exemption for contractors with under $15,000 gross annual receipts (with certificate of exemption). Commercial contractors need to register only.

mandatory_clauses.label: Minnesota contractor license disclosure
Insurance requirements
Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Misdemeanor for unlicensed work. No lien rights or lawsuit rights for unlicensed residential contractors.
notes: Minnesota requires Residential Building Contracting license for residential GC and remodeling work. Commercial contractors register only. Exempt if under $15,000 annual gross receipts. Bond: $5,000 (Class A), $15,000 (Class B). Insurance: $100,000 GL minimum required. Unlicensed residential contractors have no lien rights or lawsuit rights. Federal 3-day notice satisfies state cancellation (MN waives state notice for contracts at seller's office).
